stories.headline: Trump returns from Beijing summit without breakthrough on Iran, Taiwan arms package still pending
stories.summary: President Trump wrapped up a three-day state visit to Beijing on May 15, with nearly three hours of one-on-one talks with Xi Jinping. He returned without a deal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz or progress on a US-Iran peace agreement. Trump told reporters on the flight home that he has not yet decided whether to approve the pending $14 billion Taiwan arms package, calling it 'a very good negotiating chip'.

stories.headline: Xi warns Trump that Taiwan dispute could trigger 'clashes and even conflicts'
stories.summary: In private meetings during the summit, Xi Jinping told Trump that mishandling differences over Taiwan could push the world's two largest powers toward 'clashes and even conflicts'. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said US policy toward Taiwan remains 'unchanged' despite the warning.

stories.headline: Tensions flare in Strait of Hormuz: ship seized off UAE, another sunk near Oman
stories.summary: On May 15, a commercial vessel anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran, while a second ship was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman. The incidents mark a sharp escalation in the brinkmanship between Washington and Tehran over access to the strategic waterway.

stories.headline: Russian missile strike on Kyiv apartment building kills 24, including 3 children
stories.summary: President Zelenskyy said a Russian missile attack on a Kyiv apartment block killed 24 people, including three children — one of the deadliest single strikes on the capital in the four-year war. The attack came hours after a US-brokered three-day truce expired.

stories.headline: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next Federal Reserve chair
stories.summary: The US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve, ending months of speculation. Markets are pricing in a more hawkish posture on rates given Warsh's well-documented inflation concerns.

stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV warns AI and high-tech weapons are pushing world into 'spiral of annihilation'
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV used a major address to denounce massive investments in artificial intelligence and advanced weaponry, saying they are dragging humanity into a 'spiral of annihilation'. He called for renewed peace efforts in the Middle East and Ukraine.

stories.headline: NEA launches dengue prevention campaign as peak season begins
stories.summary: The National Environment Agency rolled out its annual dengue prevention drive as Singapore enters the traditional May-to-October peak season. NEA is urging households to follow the 'Mozzie Wipeout' B-L-O-C-K steps to remove standing water around homes. MOH separately reminded the public to follow S-A-W (Spray insecticide, Apply repellent, Wear long sleeves) in cluster areas.

stories.headline: PUB issues flash flood alert for Jurong West after morning deluge
stories.summary: The national water agency PUB alerted motorists to avoid a section of Jurong West on the morning of May 16 after intense rainfall caused localised flooding. Inter-monsoon thundery showers are forecast for most afternoons in the second half of May with daily highs around 33-35°C.

stories.headline: Straits Times: Singapore plots strategy to stand out in global AI race
stories.summary: The Straits Times features Singapore's positioning in the increasingly crowded global AI sector, focusing on talent attraction, sovereign compute, and use-case specialisation as ways the city-state aims to compete with the US and China while keeping a Southeast Asian focus.

stories.headline: New National Dengue Clinical Guideline now in force
stories.summary: Singapore's updated National Dengue Clinical Guideline, published on 31 March 2026, is now the reference for clinicians as case numbers rise with the wet season. The guideline covers diagnosis and management across the febrile, critical and recovery phases, with special protocols for the elderly, pregnant women, infants and children.

stories.headline: Inter-monsoon weather: thundery showers and Sumatra squalls expected
stories.summary: MSS forecasts short-duration thundery showers on most afternoons through the rest of May, occasionally extending into the evening. Pre-dawn Sumatra squalls with gusty winds are expected on a few days. Daily highs 33-34°C, occasionally touching 35°C.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active – truce expired, daily strikes continuing

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine
War of attrition continues; Russia makes slow incremental gains in the east while taking heavy losses
Russian forces suffer a net loss of 45 square miles of Ukrainian territory — a notable reversal from previous months
Russia and Ukraine each announce competing unilateral ceasefires that fail to hold
US President Trump brokers a 72-hour truce overlapping with Russia's Victory Day
Putin publicly suggests Russia's war 'may be coming to an end' and offers to meet Zelenskyy in a third country
Truce expires; Russia fires more than 200 drones overnight at Ukraine. April set a record with 8,000+ drones launched.
Ukraine strikes gas facilities in Russia's Orenburg region, 1,500 km from the border, in retaliation
Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment building kills 24 including 3 children — one of the deadliest of the war

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran Conflict & Strait of Hormuz
theatres.status: Ceasefire holding but Strait of Hormuz still contested

timeline.event: US and Israel launch air war on Iran; Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei assassinated
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed' and threatens to attack any ship attempting passage
Iran lets seven stranded Malaysian ships pass, signalling case-by-case exceptions for 'friendly' nations
US, Iran and Israel agree to a ceasefire after more than five weeks of fighting
US begins simultaneous naval blockade of Iranian ports, creating a 'dual blockade' situation
US declares offensive phase over; Tehran says it will not give up control of Hormuz
Trump's Beijing summit with Xi fails to produce a breakthrough on reopening Hormuz
Ship anchored off UAE is seized and taken toward Iran; a second ship attacked and sunk near Oman
Hamas attack on Israel triggers Gaza war; cumulative Palestinian deaths since now exceed 72,000 per Gaza health ministry
Israel-Hamas ceasefire signed for Gaza
Israel and Lebanon enter formal ceasefire
Israeli killings in Lebanon rising sharply; analysts question whether ceasefire still meaningfully exists
US State Department announces 45-day extension of Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
Hours after the extension, Israel launches its largest airstrikes of the war, killing more than 350 people
Israeli official says strike target Haddad was 'undermining Trump's Gaza plan'

stories.headline: WHO: 11 cases, 3 deaths in MV Hondius Andes virus outbreak
stories.summary: As of May 13, the World Health Organization counts 11 cases linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak — eight laboratory-confirmed, two probable, one inconclusive — with three deaths, giving a case fatality ratio of 27%. WHO assesses the global risk as low and continues to monitor the situation.

stories.headline: WHO working hypothesis: virus boarded the ship with the first case, then spread person-to-person
stories.summary: Investigators now believe the index case acquired Andes virus on land in South America before boarding, with onboard transmission then occurring between people. Andes is the only known hantavirus to spread human-to-human, and outbreaks typically carry case fatality rates of 20-40%.

stories.headline: MV Hondius docked in Tenerife; 147 passengers repatriated to six countries plus Canada
stories.summary: After receiving Spanish health authority approval, the ship departed for Tenerife on May 6 with additional medical resources on board, arriving May 10. The 147 remaining passengers and crew were disembarked and flown home on evacuation flights.

stories.headline: CDC classifies outbreak as 'Level 3' emergency response; no US cases linked yet
stories.summary: The US Centers for Disease Control elevated the outbreak to a Level 3 emergency response — its second-highest tier. No cases of Andes virus have been confirmed in the United States as a result of the MV Hondius cluster.

stories.headline: Operator to announce by week's end when Hondius will return to service
stories.summary: The cruise ship operator said it expects to announce by the end of the week when the MV Hondius will resume sailings. Deep-cleaning and ongoing investigations into the source of exposure on land are continuing.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN flaw CVE-2026-20182 added to CISA KEV; federal agencies must patch by May 17
stories.summary: CISA added a maximum-severity authentication bypass in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. A successful exploit lets attackers log in as a high-privileged internal account and manipulate the SD-WAN fabric. Cisco attributes active exploitation with high confidence to threat cluster UAT-8616. Federal agencies must patch by May 17, 2026.

stories.headline: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 weaponised against governments and MSPs in 5 countries
stories.summary: A CRLF-injection authentication bypass in cPanel/WHM is being used to attack government and military targets in the Philippines and Laos, plus MSPs and hosting providers in Canada, South Africa and the US. Attackers manipulate the whostmgrsession cookie to inject 'user=root' into session files. A Go-based '.sorry' Linux ransomware has been deployed; Censys counted 8,859 exposed hosts, 7,135 confirmed running cPanel/WHM.

stories.headline: Apache HTTP/2 double-free CVE-2026-23918 enables DoS and potential RCE
stories.summary: A double-free in mod_http2 of Apache httpd 2.4.66 can be triggered by sending an HTTP/2 HEADERS frame followed immediately by RST_STREAM. DoS is trivially achievable; RCE requires an info-leak and heap spray to redirect a pool cleanup pointer to system(). Fix: upgrade to 2.4.67. Disabling mod_http2 is a temporary workaround. No confirmed in-the-wild RCE yet, but opportunistic DoS likely.

stories.headline: Palo Alto Networks warns of actively exploited firewall RCE zero-day CVE-2026-0300
stories.summary: A buffer overflow in Palo Alto PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code as root via specially crafted packets sent to internet-exposed devices. Palo Alto urges immediate emergency patching of all exposed perimeter firewalls.

stories.headline: Microsoft Exchange spoofing flaw CVE-2026-42897 exploitable via crafted email
stories.summary: An XSS-driven spoofing bug in on-prem Microsoft Exchange Server can be triggered by a specially crafted email, enabling attackers to impersonate users or trigger client-side actions. Part of Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday — the first since June 2024 with no actively exploited zero-days, but several severe RCEs (DNS, Netlogon, 4× Word) were addressed across 138 fixes.

stories.headline: Windows BitLocker zero-day exposes encrypted drives; PoC public
stories.summary: Researchers released a proof-of-concept for a Windows BitLocker zero-day that allows an attacker with physical access to read protected drives. The flaw has significant implications for laptop fleets in regulated industries where BitLocker is the primary at-rest encryption control.

stories.headline: West Pharmaceutical Services still recovering nearly a week after ransomware hit
stories.summary: Global drug-delivery systems manufacturer West Pharmaceutical Services remains in active recovery nearly seven days after a ransomware attack disrupted operations. The incident underscores continued ransomware risk to medical supply chains.
